notes: Arturia Augmented GRAND PIANO combines high-quality grand piano samples with a synthesis layer for a hybrid instrument ranging from pure acoustic piano to evolved electronic textures built on a piano foundation. The acoustic layer captures the full dynamic range, string resonance, and mechanical character of a concert grand, while the synth layer augments it with pads, wavetables, or other synthesis textures. Sustain resonance is a distinctive control — it adjusts the level of sympathetic string resonance heard when the sustain pedal is held, which is one of the most characteristic aspects of an acoustic grand piano's sound. The blend control allows seamless transitions from purely acoustic to hybrid cinematic piano textures.

Modulation.description: Blend/Morph — sweeps between the Acoustic grand piano layer and the Synth layer

Sustain.description: Sustain pedal — holds notes and engages full sympathetic string resonance

Sostenuto.description: Sostenuto pedal — sustains only notes depressed at the moment of pedalling

Acoustic Layer.description: Acoustic piano layer level — level of the grand piano sample recordings

Synth Layer.description: Synth layer level — level of the synthesis component

Filter Cutoff.description: Filter cutoff — tonal shaping of the combined output; subtle movement adds expression to the synth layer

Attack.description: Amplitude envelope attack — applies primarily to the synth layer; longer attacks create slow pad swells beneath the piano

Release.description: Amplitude envelope release — tail length; longer for ambient, sustaining textures

Sustain Resonance.description: Sustain string resonance — level of sympathetic string resonance when the sustain pedal is held; higher = more acoustic grand character

Reverb.description: Reverb send level — concert hall reverb enhances both the acoustic and synth layers

Motion Rate.description: Motion/LFO rate — automated parameter animation speed for evolving hybrid textures
